_doc: CANONICAL source of truth for the per-agent plugin manifests. Hand-edit THIS file; the .claude-plugin/ and .codex-plugin/ manifests are GENERATED from it by `node build.mjs` (adapters/*.mjs). `node build.mjs --check` fails if a generated manifest drifts from this source. See docs/strategy/agent-harness-overhaul-2026-07-09/ws-a-design.md.

claude.description: Linlin's curated Claude Code configuration: workflow rules, skills, hooks, plugin recommendations, tooling preferences, and project templates. Install once, then run /init-agent-config in any new project to scaffold it with the relevant subset.
notes: Claude Code uses .claude-plugin/plugin.json. Codex uses .codex-plugin/plugin.json and root hooks.json; keeping them separate preserves Claude Code behavior while making the adapter discoverable by Codex.
When either plugin spec changes, update the corresponding manifest without removing fields used by the other agent's discovery path.

codex.description: Codex adapter for Linlin's agent-harness workflows, reusable skills, project setup guidance, hooks, recommendations, tooling preferences, templates, and safe Codex update repair.

interface.longDescription: Use agent-harness from Codex without changing the original Claude Code setup. The plugin exposes Codex-compatible setup, verification, visual review, research critique, privacy redaction, system cleanup, long-running task, safe Codex update, and cross-agent adaptation workflows while keeping Claude-specific files isolated.
defaultPrompt: Apply agent-harness to this project for Codex
Activate agent-harness as my default local Codex plugin
Repair a Codex CLI update that failed because latest release assets were unavailable

opencode._doc: opencode has NO static plugin manifest (plugins are JS). We emit a real opencode.json (JSONC-compatible). rules -> instructions[]; hooks -> plugin[] (names only; the hook LOGIC is a separate JS port, tracked in WS-A A.2 / WS-C). opencode reads .claude/skills/ natively, so skills need no translation. model/small_model are editable defaults (task 10). Source: opencode.ai/docs.
opencode._plugin_note: opencode auto-discovers local plugins from .opencode/plugins/*.js (e.g. review-gate.js), so they are NOT listed here. `plugin[]` is only for npm-published plugin names; we have none, so it is empty (an earlier version wrongly listed non-existent npm names = dead refs).
